title: Semantic HTML5
summary: Semantic elements (`header`, `nav`, `main`, `article`, `section`, `footer`) communicate structure to assistive tech and search engines.
content:
## Landmarks

Use **one** `main` per page. Headings should outline content (`h1` → `h2`…) without skipping levels carelessly.

## When to use article vs section

- **article** — self-contained piece (post, card with permalink).
- **section** — thematic grouping inside a document.

code_examples.code:
…

Guide

Tips

…

related_questions.preview: Why semantic HTML matters for a11y

title: Forms and validation
summary: HTML5 adds input types, built-in validation attributes, and associations between labels and controls for accessibility.
content:
## Labels

Every control should have an accessible name via `<label for>` or `aria-label` / `aria-labelledby`.

## Validation

Use `required`, `pattern`, `min`/`max`, and server-side validation—never rely on client validation alone.

code_examples.code:
<form>
  <label for="email">Email</label>
  <input
    id="email"
    name="email"
    type="email"
    required
    autocomplete="email"
  />
  <button type="submit">Subscribe</button>
</form>

title: Web Storage API (localStorage & sessionStorage)
summary: Persist key–value strings in the browser—`localStorage` survives tabs/restarts; `sessionStorage` is scoped to the tab session.
content:
## Limits

Same-origin only; ~5MB typical quota (engine-dependent); **strings only**—`JSON.stringify` for objects.

## Security

Not secret storage—any script on the origin can read it; avoid tokens unless hardened.

## Pattern

Feature-detect `window.localStorage` and wrap `try/catch` for private mode/quota errors.

title: HTML input types & built-in validation
summary: Specialized `type` values give native UX—keyboard hints, pickers, and constraint validation without plugins.
content:
## Common types

`email`, `url`, `tel`, `number`, `date`, `time`, `datetime-local`, `range`, `color`, `search`, `file`.

## Behavior

Browsers show suitable controls and lightweight validation on submit; **always validate on the server**.

## Era

Central to the **better forms** story (~2012 onward), refined through ~2020.

code_examples.code:
<label for="e">Email</label>
<input id="e" type="email" name="email" required />

<label for="d">Start date</label>
<input id="d" type="date" name="start" />

<label for="v">Volume</label>
<input id="v" type="range" min="0" max="100" value="50" />

title: Native form validation attributes
summary: `required`, `min`/`max`, `pattern`, `step`, `maxlength`, and form-level `novalidate` integrate with the Constraint Validation API—often no JS for basic UX.
content:
## JS hooks

`element.checkValidity()`, `reportValidity()`, `setCustomValidity()` for messages.

## Limits

Accessibility still needs errors tied with **`aria-invalid`** and descriptions—don’t rely on browser chrome alone.

## Timeline

Part of the **2016–2020** push for richer native forms.

code_examples.code:
<form>
  <input type="text" name="code" required pattern="[A-Z]{3}-[0-9]{4}" />
  <input type="number" name="age" min="18" max="120" />
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>

title: placeholder & autofocus
summary: `placeholder` hints at expected input but is **not** a label replacement. `autofocus` moves keyboard focus on load—use sparingly.
content:
## A11y

Screen readers may skip placeholder text when the field is filled—always provide a visible **label**.

## autofocus

Avoid on pages where users expect to scroll first or when it steals focus from skip links.

## Mobile

Placeholder contrast must still meet WCAG.

code_examples.code:
<label for="name">Name</label>
<input
  id="name"
  name="name"
  placeholder="Enter name"
  autocomplete="name"
  autofocus
/>

title: Drag and Drop API
summary: `draggable="true"` plus event handlers (`dragstart`, `dragover`, `drop`) enable file lists and board UIs—often paired with file APIs.
content:
## Defaults

Links/images may drag natively; call **`preventDefault`** on `dragover` to allow drop.

## A11y

Provide **keyboard** alternatives—DnD alone is not sufficient.

## Mobile

Touch behavior differs; test or offer alternate flows.

code_examples.code:
Drag me
Drop here

title: Geolocation API
summary: `navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition` reads device location after user permission—HTTPS required in production browsers.
content:
## Privacy

Always explain **why** you need location; handle denial and timeouts.

## Accuracy

High accuracy costs battery—tune `enableHighAccuracy`.

## Note

This is a **JavaScript Web API**, used alongside HTML pages—not an HTML tag.

title: contenteditable
summary: `contenteditable="true"` makes regions user-editable—quick prototypes; production rich text usually needs a proper editor and sanitization.
content:
## Risks

Pasting HTML can inject unsafe markup—**sanitize** before saving.

## A11y

Expose role/name and toolbar patterns if mimicking an editor.

## Alternative

Hide complex WYSIWYG behind components with tested behavior.

code_examples.code:
<section contenteditable="true" aria-label="Notes">
  Edit this text inline.
</section>

title: Lazy loading with loading="lazy"
summary: Defer offscreen `` and `
content:
## Rules

Great for below-the-fold media; **avoid** `lazy` on hero/LCP images—use **`fetchpriority`** instead.

## Timeline

Landmark **performance & UX** feature in the **2021–2023** era alongside responsive images.

title: <picture> & responsive images
summary: `` chooses sources by media/type; combine with `srcset`/`sizes` on `` for resolution switching.
content:
## Use cases

Art direction (crop differs), **WebP/AVIF** with JPEG fallback, dark/light assets.

## Performance

Give explicit **`width`/`height`** or CSS **`aspect-ratio`** to reduce CLS.

title: <details> & <summary>
summary: Built-in disclosure widget—expand/collapse without JS for FAQs and filters.
content:
## A11y

Native disclosure semantics in supporting browsers; enhance keyboard tests.

## Styling

`::marker` / shadow DOM quirks vary—polyfills rarely needed for basics.

code_examples.code:
More info

Extra content revealed when open.

title: <template>
summary: Inert fragment holder—clone its `.content` into the document when rendering lists or widgets.
content:
## Why

Markup stays off-screen without display hacks; safe staging before attach.

## Usage

`document.importNode(template.content, true)` then append.

title: Intersection Observer (with HTML elements)
summary: Efficiently observe when elements enter/exit the viewport—pairs with HTML for infinite scroll, lazy mounts, and analytics. Implemented in JavaScript, driven by DOM nodes.
content:
## vs scroll listeners

Avoid main-thread scroll thrash—observer batches callbacks.

## Pattern

Observe sentinel nodes or cards; unobserve after load.

## Note

API is **JavaScript**—listed here because scripts target concrete HTML elements.

title: <dialog> — native modal
summary: The `<dialog>` element plus `.showModal()` provides focus trapping, backdrop, and ESC close in supporting browsers—often replaces lightweight modal libraries.
content:
## Methods

`showModal()` vs `show()`—modal captures focus and displays ::backdrop.

## Progressive enhancement

Fallback dialog patterns for older engines.

## Era

Major **modern HTML** enhancement for accessible overlays (~2024 Baseline momentum).

code_examples.code: Modal content

title: popover & Popover API
summary: `popover` attribute + `popovertarget` buttons give declarative menus/tooltips—layers above anchor positioning for polished chrome.
content:
## Behavior

Light-dismiss, focus management basics—verify cross-browser.

## Pairing

Works with **`dialog`** and CSS anchor positioning where supported.

code_examples.code: Menu content

title: inert attribute
summary: `inert` removes elements from interaction and tab order—ideal behind modals or during loading states.
content:
## vs disabled

Broader than disabling inputs—whole subtrees become non-interactive.

## Pairing

Use when **`dialog.showModal`** already traps focus; `inert` helps background content.

## Support

Progressive enhancement—test Safari/Firefox matrices.

code_examples.code:
...
Disabled UI behind modal

title: fetchpriority on images & scripts
summary: `fetchpriority="high"` hints critical hero assets; `low` deprioritizes decorative images—helps schedulers alongside `loading`.
content:
## Usage

Mark **one** likely LCP image high; avoid marking everything.

## Relation

Distinct from **`preload`** link—different mechanism.

title: decoding on images
summary: `decoding="async"` lets the browser decode images off the main thread path where supported—often paired with `loading` and dimensions.
content:
## Values

`sync` vs `async` vs `auto`—pick async for below-fold media.

## CLS

Still provide width/height or CSS aspect-ratio.

title: inputmode attribute
summary: Hints which virtual keyboard to show (`numeric`, `decimal`, `tel`, etc.)—especially on mobile—without changing `type="text"` semantics when you need pattern control.
content:
## Distinction

Not the same as `type="number"`—still validate and sanitize server-side.

## Accessibility

Combine with labels and errors announced to AT.

code_examples.code:
<label for="otp">Code</label>
<input
  id="otp"
  type="text"
  inputmode="numeric"
  pattern="[0-9]*"
  maxlength="6"
  autocomplete="one-time-code"
/>

title: autocomplete tokens
summary: Rich `autocomplete` values (`email`, `current-password`, `one-time-code`, section prefixes) help password managers and SMS OTP autofill.
content:
## Security

Never autocomplete secrets you don’t intend stored—pair policies with product.

## OTP

`one-time-code` assists SMS/WebOTP flows on supporting devices.

code_examples.code:
<input type="email" name="email" autocomplete="email" />
<input type="password" name="password" autocomplete="current-password" />
<input type="text" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="one-time-code" />

title: Web Components (custom elements)
summary: Define reusable `<my-component>` tags with Custom Elements, Shadow DOM, and `<slot>`—framework-agnostic UI capsules.
content:
## Fit

Design systems embedded in legacy pages or micro-frontends.

## Trade-offs

SSR/hydration story differs from React—plan serialization.

## Basics

`customElements.define('my-card', class extends HTMLElement { … })`.

code_examples.code: <my-badge>New</my-badge>
